content.body: i need some help understanding the Link stuff in https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sup-availablenamedtimezoneidentifiers

if i do `new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en',{timeZone:'America/Kentucky/Louisville'}).resolvedOptions().timeZone`, and there's `Link	America/Kentucky/Louisville	America/Louisville` in the `backward` file in tzdb, does 402 say the result _must_ be `America/Kentucky/Louisville`?
content.formatted_body: if i do new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en',{timeZone:'America/Kentucky/Louisville'}).resolvedOptions().timeZone, and there's Link America/Kentucky/Louisville America/Louisville in the backward file in tzdb, does 402 say the result must be America/Kentucky/Louisville?

content.body: When Temporal is merged along with the Time Zone Canonicalization proposal, the behavior will be to retain the input time zone name. CC justingrant, can you elaborate?
content.formatted_body: When Temporal is merged along with the Time Zone Canonicalization proposal, the behavior will be to retain the input time zone name. CC @justingrant:matrix.org, can you elaborate?

content.body: am i correct in interpreting that to mean "currently 402 requires canonicalization, but once temporal merges input tz name is retained"?
